description: MEP Bridge lightweight tool descriptor registry for AI Adapter, Workbench, SDK and CLI discovery.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_selected_element_details
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get selected element details
descriptors.description: Read details for the current Archicad selection through MEPBridge, including type, recognition metadata and AABB geometry, with official selection readback available for comparison.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.move_selected_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge move selected elements
descriptors.description: Move the current Archicad selection by a delta offset (small displacement <= 5000mm per axis). This is D3 P0 confirmed-run mutation: requires dry-run → user confirmation → execute → readback → reverse restore.

deltaMm.description: Extract x/y/z displacement from text, supports 'x=300,y=-200,z=100' and directional words like '上/下/左/右/前/后'

safetyConstraints.reason: D3 P0 limits small displacement to <= 5000mm per axis to reduce risk.

steps.description: Read current selection with AABB (preState)
Validate displacement <= 5000mm per axis
Execute MoveSelectedElements with dryRun=true
Show dry-run result to user, require explicit confirmation
Execute MoveSelectedElements with dryRun=false (user confirmed)
Read selection again and verify AABB offset matches deltaMm
Execute inverse move (-deltaMm) to restore original position
Read selection final AABB and verify it matches preState

notes: Selection must remain the same or be re-resolved from preState GUIDs
Rollback is also a confirmed-run operation
User input uses mm (building default unit).
MEPBridge.MoveSelectedElements converts deltaMm to meters internally.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.modify_properties_selected_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge modify properties of selected elements
descriptors.description: Modify non-structural Archicad Properties of the current selection. This is D3 P1 confirmed-run mutation: requires preState capture → safety check → dry-run → user confirmation → execute → readback → restore to original values.

properties.description: Extract property name/value pairs from text

safetyConstraints.reason: D3 P1 limits modifications to non-structural Archicad Properties only.
safetyConstraints.validation: Pre-flight check enforces property type whitelist and existence validation.

steps.description: Read current selection with properties (preState)
Validate property types (whitelist) and existence

validation.check: All properties in whitelist and exist on elements

steps.description: Execute EditSelectedElements with dryRun=true
Show property changes (before → after), require explicit confirmation
steps.userAction: User approves or rejects property modifications

steps.description: Execute EditSelectedElements with dryRun=false (user confirmed)
Read selection properties again and verify values match expected
Execute EditSelectedElements to restore original property values
Read selection properties final and verify they match preState

validation.pass: Successfully restored to original values, D3 P1 round-trip passed

notes: Selection must remain the same or be re-resolved from preState GUIDs
Rollback is also a confirmed-run operation

descriptors.name: mepbridge.copy_elements_confirmed
descriptors.title: MEPBridge copy elements with confirmation
descriptors.description: Copy selected elements (< 10 elements) with full confirmed-run loop: preState → safety check → dry-run → user confirmation → execute → readback → cleanup (rollback) → cleanup verification. This is D3 P2 confirmed-run mutation.

safetyConstraints.reason: D3 P2 limits small batch copy to < 10 elements to reduce risk.
safetyConstraints.validation: Pre-flight check enforces source element count < 10.

steps.description: Read current selection as source elements (preState)
Validate source element count < 10
Execute CopyElements with dryRun=true (if supported) or simulate preview
steps.note: If CopyElements doesn't support dry-run, simulate by showing offset preview

steps.description: Show copy details (source → target offset), require explicit confirmation
steps.userAction: User approves or rejects copy operation

steps.description: Execute CopyElements with dryRun=false (user confirmed)
Read created elements and verify GUIDs exist

validation.check: All createdGuids exist and have valid AABB
validation.fail: Copy failed, some GUIDs not found

steps.description: Execute DeleteElements to remove created elements
Verify created elements are deleted and source elements intact

validation.check: createdGuids not found, sourceGuids still exist
validation.pass: Successfully cleaned up, D3 P2 round-trip passed

notes: Rollback uses DeleteElements to remove all created GUIDs
Created GUIDs must be captured during execute step
Cleanup is also a confirmed-run operation

descriptors.name: mepbridge.ping
descriptors.title: MEPBridge Ping
descriptors.description: Check MEPBridge Add-On connection status.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_project_info
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get project info
descriptors.description: Get current Archicad project metadata (name, path, location, story structure etc.). Read-only native query.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_stories
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get stories
descriptors.description: Get all stories (floor levels) in the current Archicad project. Read-only native query.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.set_stories
descriptors.title: MEPBridge batch rename stories
descriptors.description: Batch rename existing Archicad stories by index. Prevalidates the full request, supports dry-run and confirmation, and restores earlier names if a later rename fails.

stories.description: Non-empty array of {index,name} objects for existing stories

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_libraries
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get libraries
descriptors.description: Get all loaded Archicad libraries in the current project. Read-only native query.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_hotlinks
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get hotlinks
descriptors.description: Get all hotlinks in the current Archicad project. Read-only native query.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_grid_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get grid elements
descriptors.description: Enumerate all grid elements (axis/grid lines) in the Archicad project. Grid elements are Object variants identified by APIVarId_GridElement ('GREL'), not a separate ElemTypeID. Returns guid/type/floorIndex/variationId/layerName per element, with optional AABB. Read-only native query. Language-agnostic (no layer name dependency).

descriptors.name: mepbridge.scan_structural_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge scan structural elements
descriptors.description: Read-only scan of Wall/Column/Beam/Slab/Roof elements and return AABB geometry. Does not modify the model.

types.description: Optional element type filter, defaults to [Wall,Column,Beam,Slab,Roof]

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_available_sizes
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get available pipe sizes
descriptors.description: Query available pipe sizes / diameter reference table for a given MEP domain (Piping by default).

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_available_systems
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get available MEP systems
descriptors.description: Query available MEP systems across Piping / Ventilation / CableCarrier domains.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_element_property_definitions
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get element property definitions
descriptors.description: Query available Archicad Property definitions for the current selection. Read-only.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.delete_mep_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge delete MEP route elements
descriptors.description: Delete specified MEP route elements by GUID. Only works for MEP Routing Elements (pipes/ducts/cable carriers). For deleting architectural elements (walls/columns/beams/slabs/roofs/doors/windows), use mepbridge.delete_elements instead. Irreversible mutation, requires confirmation.

guids.description: MEP route element GUIDs to delete

descriptors.name: mepbridge.delete_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge delete elements (universal)
descriptors.description: Delete any type of Archicad element by GUID (walls/columns/beams/slabs/roofs/doors/windows/MEP routes/etc). Universal deletion command - unlike DeleteMEPElements which only works for MEP routes. Added 2026-06-29 after AI auto-drawing floorplan found DeleteMEPElements cannot delete architectural walls. Irreversible high-risk mutation, requires confirmation.

guids.description: Element GUIDs to delete (any type: Wall/Column/Beam/Slab/Roof/Door/Window/MEP Route)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_pipe
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create pipe
descriptors.description: Create a new MEP pipe route between two 3D points. Mutation, requires confirmation.

waypoints.description: Pipe route waypoints [{x,y,z}] in Archicad model coordinates; start/end input is normalized to waypoints by the server

start.description: Legacy start point [x,y,z]; normalized to waypoints

end.description: Legacy end point [x,y,z]; normalized to waypoints

referenceId.description: Pipe diameter reference ID from GetAvailableSizes

diameterMm.description: Pipe diameter in millimeters, extracted from '100mm水管' / 'DN20' / '直径100mm' etc. Falls back to 22.0mm if not specified.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_pipe_system
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create pipe system (main + branches)
descriptors.description: Create a complete piping system with main route and multiple branch routes in one request. Mutation, requires confirmation.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_mep_element_info
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get MEP element info
descriptors.description: Read back MEP Routing Element info by GUID: domain, polyline, segments, referenceId, etc.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_duct
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create duct (HVAC)
descriptors.description: Create a new MEP duct route between two 3D points using rectangular cross section. Mutation, requires confirmation.

waypoints.description: Duct route waypoints [{x,y,z}] in Archicad model coordinates

width.description: Duct width in meters, e.g. from '300mm风管' or '宽300高200'

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_cable_carrier
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create cable carrier / tray
descriptors.description: Create a new MEP cable carrier (tray/ladder) route between two 3D points using rectangular cross section. Mutation, requires confirmation.

waypoints.description: Cable carrier route waypoints [{x,y,z}] in Archicad model coordinates

width.description: Carrier width in meters, e.g. from '300mm桥架'

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_wall
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create wall (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a straight wall between two 2D points using native Archicad API (API_WallID). Architectural element, not MEP. Supports dryRun preview and confirmRequired execution. V2 H1.1 channel A (MEPBridge C++ preferred).

start.description: Wall start point {x,y} in meters (Archicad model coords)

thickness.description: Wall thickness in meters, e.g. from '240mm墙' → 0.24

height.description: Wall height in meters relative to floor, e.g. from '3米高墙' → 3.0

floorIndex.description: Story index (0-based), defaults to current story

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_column
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create column (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a vertical column at a 2D position using native Archicad API (API_ColumnID). Supports height and rotation. V2 H1.1 channel A.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_beam
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create beam (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a straight beam between two 2D points using native Archicad API (API_BeamID). V2 H1.1 channel A.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_slab
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create slab (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a slab from a polygon outline using native Archicad API (API_SlabID). Requires at least 3 vertices. V2 H1.1 channel A.

polygon.description: Polygon vertices [{x,y}] in meters, at least 3 points

level.description: Reference level offset from floor in meters

descriptors.name: mepbridge.rotate_selected_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge rotate selected elements
descriptors.description: Rotate currently selected elements around a center point by a given angle. Uses APIEdit_Rotate. V2 H1.3 channel A.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.mirror_selected_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge mirror selected elements
descriptors.description: Mirror currently selected elements across an axis line. Uses APIEdit_Mirror. Supports copy mode (keep original). V2 H1.3 channel A.

axisStart.description: Mirror axis start point {x,y} in meters

axisEnd.description: Mirror axis end point {x,y} in meters

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_door
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create door (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a door opening in an existing wall using native Archicad API (API_WindowID with isWindow=false). Requires a host wall GUID. V2 H1.1 channel A.

width.description: Door opening width in meters, e.g. '0.9米宽的门' → 0.9

height.description: Door opening height in meters, e.g. '2.1米高' → 2.1

refPos.description: Position along wall from start point in meters

libPartIndex.description: Library part index for door object

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_window
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create window (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a window opening in an existing wall using native Archicad API (API_WindowID with isWindow=true). Requires a host wall GUID. V2 H1.1 channel A.

refPos.description: Position along wall from start point in meters

sillHeight.description: Sill height above floor in meters

libPartIndex.description: Library part index for window object

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_roof
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create roof (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a roof from a polygon outline using native Archicad API (API_RoofID) with pitch angle support. Requires at least 3 vertices. V2 H1.1 channel A.

vertices.description: Roof polygon vertices [{x,y}] in meters, at least 3 points forming closed shape

pitchAngle.description: Roof pitch angle in degrees (0=flat, default=30)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_stair
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create stair (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a stair from baseline + totalHeight + stepNum using native Archicad API (API_StairID). V2 channel A.

start.description: Baseline start point {x,y} in meters (used when waypoints not provided)

end.description: Baseline end point {x,y} in meters (used when waypoints not provided)

waypoints.description: Baseline polyline vertices [{x,y}] for U-shaped/curved stair. Overrides start/end when provided (min 2 points)

totalHeight.description: Total stair height in meters (e.g. 3.0 for one floor)

baseLevel.description: Base plane elevation in meters relative to the home story (optional)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_object
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create object (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a GDL object at a position with optional library part name and rotation. API_ObjectID. V2 channel A.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_lamp
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create lamp (architectural)
descriptors.description: Create a lamp/light fixture at a position. API_LampID (= API_ObjectType typedef). V2 channel A.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_mesh
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create mesh (terrain)
descriptors.description: Create a terrain mesh from a polygon outline. API_MeshID. V2 channel A.

polygon.description: Mesh polygon vertices [{x,y}] in meters, at least 3 points

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_zone
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create zone (room)
descriptors.description: Create a zone/room from a polygon outline with name and height. API_ZoneID. V2 channel A.

polygon.description: Zone polygon vertices [{x,y}] in meters, at least 3 points

descriptors.name: mepbridge.viewport_capture
descriptors.title: MEPBridge viewport capture (visual feedback)
descriptors.description: Capture current viewport as structured visual snapshot (view type + visible elements AABB list + bounds). Used by H4 visual feedback loop for LLM to verify execution results. Read-only, no model modification. V2 H4.1.

includeImage.description: Capture PNG screenshot as base64 for LLM multimodal vision (default=false, H4.1)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_element_mesh
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get element 3D mesh
descriptors.description: Get 3D geometry vertices for specified elements by GUID. Returns AABB + key vertices (wall start/end, column base/top, slab polygon) + thickness/height. Used by H4.4 for precise geometric verification. Read-only. V2 H4.4.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.auto_route_pipe
descriptors.title: MEPBridge auto route pipe (dry-run obstacle avoidance)
descriptors.description: Auto-route a MEP pipe between waypoints with obstacle avoidance. Dry-run only: computes collision-free plan against structural obstacles, reports blockedBy segments and routing preference. LLM uses this to plan pipe routes that avoid walls/columns/beams. V2 MEP core.

waypoints.description: Pipe route waypoints [{x,y,z}] in meters; at least 2 points required

clearanceMm.description: Clearance distance from obstacles in mm (required, >=0). Extracted from '避让50mm' / 'clearance 100mm'

diameterMm.description: Pipe diameter in mm, extracted from 'DN20' / '100mm管'

referenceId.description: Pipe cross-section reference ID from GetAvailableSizes

mepSystemName.description: MEP system name, e.g. 'Domestic Cold Water'

descriptors.name: mepbridge.solve_connections
descriptors.title: MEPBridge solve pipe connections (merge/tee/cross detection)
descriptors.description: Detect and optionally apply logical connections between MEP piping routes at shared points. Supports merge (2 routes), tee (3 routes), cross (4 routes, dry-run only). LLM uses this to connect independently-created pipe segments into a network. V2 MEP core.

routeGuids.description: Array of route GUIDs to connect (min 2). Extracted from selection or explicit GUIDs

routeGuid.description: Single route GUID (legacy, normalized to routeGuids)

apply.description: If true, apply merge/tee connections; if false (default), dry-run detect only

crossMode.description: Cross fitting mode: none|dry-run|place (place not implemented)

tol.description: Tolerance for grouping nearby route endpoints (default 0.001m)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_element_properties
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get element properties by GUID
descriptors.description: Read Archicad Properties of a route/element by GUID. Returns property values with definition names, groups, and types. LLM uses this to inspect element attributes (material, fire rating, system tag, etc.) before deciding modifications. V2 property readback core.

routeGuid.description: Route/element GUID to query (required). Extracted from selection or explicit GUID

propertyGuids.description: Optional specific property definition GUIDs to query

descriptors.name: mepbridge.set_element_property
descriptors.title: MEPBridge set single element property
descriptors.description: Set a single Archicad Property on a route/element by GUID. Supports lookup by propertyGuid or by groupName+propertyName. Returns previous and new value. LLM uses this for targeted single-attribute modifications (e.g. set fire rating, update material tag). V2 property write core.

routeGuid.description: Route/element GUID (required). Extracted from selection or explicit GUID

propertyGuid.description: Property definition GUID (optional, alternative to propertyName)

propertyName.description: Property name (e.g. 'Fire Rating'). Used with groupName for name-based lookup

groupName.description: Property group name (e.g. 'General'). Used with propertyName

valueString.description: New property value as string (required). e.g. 'REI-60', 'Steel', 'true'

descriptors.name: mepbridge.set_element_properties
descriptors.title: MEPBridge batch set element properties
descriptors.description: Batch set multiple Archicad Properties on a route/element by GUID in one atomic operation. Each item supports propertyGuid or groupName+propertyName lookup. Returns previous+new values per item. LLM uses this for multi-attribute updates in a single undoable transaction (e.g. set material+fire rating+system tag together). V2 property write core.

routeGuid.description: Route/element GUID (required). Extracted from selection or explicit GUID

descriptors.name: mepbridge.find_routes_by_property
descriptors.title: MEPBridge find routes by single property
descriptors.description: Find MEP routing elements matching a single property value (e.g. find all pipes with fireRating=REI-60). Supports Piping/Ventilation/CableCarrier/All domains. LLM uses this for property-based MEP route queries. V2 MEP query core.

propertyGuid.description: Property definition GUID (optional, alternative to propertyName)

groupName.description: Property group name for name-based lookup

includeProperty.description: Include matched property in results (default true)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.find_routes_by_properties
descriptors.title: MEPBridge find routes by multiple properties (AND/OR)
descriptors.description: Find MEP routing elements matching multiple property conditions with AND/OR match mode. Supports export to rows/csv. LLM uses this for complex multi-criteria MEP queries (e.g. find pipes with fireRating=REI-60 AND material=Steel). V2 MEP query core.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.set_routes_properties
descriptors.title: MEPBridge batch set properties on multiple routes
descriptors.description: Batch set properties on multiple MEP routing elements in one atomic operation. Each route item contains routeGuid + properties array. LLM uses this for bulk MEP route attribute updates (e.g. set systemCode on all selected pipes). V2 MEP write core.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.ensure_property_definitions
descriptors.title: MEPBridge ensure property definitions exist
descriptors.description: Ensure MEP metadata property definitions exist for route elements (externalId/systemCode/routeName/floor/discipline/source/Note). Creates property group + string definitions if missing. LLM uses this as a prerequisite before setting custom metadata on routes. V2 MEP property workflow.

propertyNames.description: Array of property name strings to ensure (default: externalId/systemCode/routeName/floor/discipline/source/Note)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.move_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge move elements by GUID
descriptors.description: Move MEP routing elements by explicit GUID list with delta offset (mm). Requires confirmRequired=true. LLM uses this when it has specific route GUIDs (not current selection) and needs precise positional adjustment. Alternative to MoveSelectedElements for GUID-targeted automation.

routeGuids.description: Array of route GUIDs to move (min 1). Extracted from selection or explicit GUIDs

routeGuid.description: Single route GUID (legacy, normalized to routeGuids)

deltaMm.description: Movement delta in mm {x,y,z} (required, non-zero). Extracted from 'x=300,y=-200,z=100'

confirmRequired.description: Must be true (required by C++ schema)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.edit_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge edit element properties by GUID
descriptors.description: Edit properties of MEP routing elements by explicit GUID list. Each route gets its own property array. Requires confirmRequired=true. LLM uses this when it has specific route GUIDs (not current selection) and needs to modify their properties. Alternative to EditSelectedElements for GUID-targeted automation.

routeGuids.description: Array of route GUIDs to edit (min 1)

routeGuid.description: Single route GUID (legacy, normalized to routeGuids)

confirmRequired.description: Must be true (required by C++ schema)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.change_element_geometry
descriptors.title: MEPBridge change element geometry by GUID
descriptors.description: Modify geometry of an existing element (Wall/Column/Beam) by GUID. Wall: start+end or thickness or height. Column: position or height. Beam: start+end. Uses ACAPI_Element_Change wrapped in UndoableCommand. LLM uses this to resize/reposition existing elements. P3-3, schema extended 2026-07-02.

elementGuid.description: Element GUID to modify (required). Extracted from selection or explicit GUID

start.description: New start point {x,y} for Wall/Beam (meters, optional)

end.description: New end point {x,y} for Wall/Beam (meters, optional)

thickness.description: Wall thickness in meters (optional, Wall only)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.batch_create_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge batch create elements (atomic, max 500)
descriptors.description: Batch create multiple elements of the same type (Wall/Column/Beam) in a single atomic UndoableCommand. Max 500 items per batch. Partial failures keep successfully created elements. LLM uses this for large-scale modeling (e.g. create 20 columns, build 4 walls of a room). P3-4.

elementType.description: Element type: Wall|Column|Beam (all items must be same type)

items.description: Array of element specs: Wall/Beam items need {start,end}, Column items need {position} (min 2, max 500)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_elements_by_type
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get elements by type
descriptors.description: Query all elements of a specific type from the Archicad model. Returns guid/type/floorIndex/layerIndex per element, with optional AABB. Supports Wall/Column/Beam/Slab/Roof/Door/Window/Object/Stair/MEPRoute. Native element discovery query. P4-2.

descriptors.name: mepbridge.set_selected_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge set selection (low-mutation, AI location assist)
descriptors.description: Change the current Archicad selection to the specified element GUIDs. Supports replace/add/remove modes. Helps AI locate problem elements for user confirmation. Does not modify the model. P4-3.

guids.description: Array of element GUIDs to select (min 1)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_element_geometry
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get element precise geometry (read)
descriptors.description: Get precise geometry of a single element by GUID. Returns type-specific fields: Wall→start/end/thickness/height/length, Column→position/height, Beam→start/end/length, Slab→polygon/thickness/level, Roof→polygon/thickness/baseLevel, Door/Window→width/height/owner. P4-5.

elementGuid.description: GUID of the element to query (required)

descriptors.name: mepbridge.change_opening_geometry
descriptors.title: MEPBridge change door/window geometry (low-mutation)
descriptors.description: Modify geometry of an existing door or window by GUID. Supports width/height/refPos/sillHeight(Window only)/libPartIndex. Fills the gap of ChangeElementGeometry which only supports Wall/Column/Beam. P4-7.

elementGuid.description: GUID of the door/window to modify (required)

libPartIndex.description: New library part index for style

descriptors.name: mepbridge.change_mep_route_properties
descriptors.title: MEPBridge change MEP route properties (medium-mutation)
descriptors.description: Modify properties of an existing MEP route by GUID. Supports mepSystemIndex/mepSystemName. Uses RoutingElement::Modify API. Does not modify cross-section (diameter), waypoints, or offsetFromHomeStory — those require RoutingSegment/Node level APIs (future). P4-8.

routeGuid.description: GUID of the MEP route to modify (required)

mepSystemName.description: New MEP system name (alternative to index)

offsetFromHomeStory.description: New offset from home story in meters

descriptors.name: mepbridge.get_mep_all_elements
descriptors.title: MEPBridge get all MEP elements (classified)
descriptors.description: Query all MEP elements in the model and classify by type (RoutingElement/FlexibleSegment/Branch/TakeOff/Other). Returns controlPoints for FlexibleSegments when includeGeometry=true. Uses CollectAllMEPElements() for complete coverage.

includeGeometry.description: If true, return spline control points for FlexibleSegments

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_flexible_segment
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create flexible segment (soft duct/pipe)
descriptors.description: Create an MEP flexible segment (soft hose/spline) with control points. Supports Ventilation (duct flex) and Piping (pipe flex) domains. Geometry defined by 3+ controlPoints (spline curve). Requires startOrientation and endOrientation.

controlPoints.description: Spline control points [{x,y,z}] in meters, min 2 points

descriptors.name: mepbridge.create_takeoff
descriptors.title: MEPBridge create takeoff (branch connector)
descriptors.description: Create a Takeoff (branch connector) on an existing RoutingSegment. Requires either segmentGuid or routeGuid+segmentIndex to identify the host segment, plus distanceFromBeginning along the segment and optional rotationAngle.

routeGuid.description: GUID of the parent RoutingElement (used with segmentIndex)

segmentIndex.description: Segment index within the route (0-based), default 0

distanceFromBeginning.description: Distance along segment from start in meters

rotationAngle.description: Rotation around segment axis in radians, default 0
